content: agent1: implementing dry_run option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. Will touch Image.py's save() method.

Ack - we both touch Image.save(). I'll add a dry_run param (early-return before writing bytes, but still run the encoder to catch errors). I'll work directly in Image.py; will coop-recv before finalizing to check your diff location so we don't clobber each other's edits. Suggest: since we both edit the same function body, let's each make minimal, localized edits and share exact line ranges before final submission.

Done with dry_run feature. Signature is now: def save(self, fp, format=None, *, dry_run=False, **params) — I added '*,' after format making dry_run and everything after keyword-only. If you add backup/backup_suffix after dry_run in the signature, they'll also be keyword-only (that's fine, save() is almost always called with kwargs). Body change: right after 'created = False', I added 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: ...' and guarded the two fp.close() calls with 'and not dry_run'. My patch.txt is already written (only touches save()'s signature/docstring + that one if/elif block + two close guards). Let me know when you're done so I can double check for overlap before you finalize.

Heads up: I changed 'if open_fp:' to 'elif open_fp:' (since dry_run branch comes first with 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()'). Your backup block should go inside that elif branch, right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' - same as before, just note it's now 'elif' not 'if'. This also means when dry_run=True, backup logic is skipped entirely (no file touched), which seems like correct behavior. Also note I added '*,' in the save() signature after 'format=None' making dry_run and all following params keyword-only - if you add backup/backup_suffix as explicit params they'll need to go after dry_run (still keyword-only, no functional difference for typical **kwargs-style calls). Sounds like no real conflicts, just wanted to flag the elif change.
